skill.name: deep-research
skill.description: Run Gemini-based deep research workflows for market analysis, competitive research, literature reviews, technical research, and due diligence.
skill.summary: Guide autonomous Gemini research tasks with cited reports, progress tracking, and follow-up workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are mostly Markdown examples for installing dependencies and running a local research script. The network and environment findings are legitimate for a Gemini research workflow, but users should treat the API key and referenced script path as sensitive.

medium_findings.title: External Command Workflow Requires Review
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to install dependencies and run python3 scripts/research.py for research tasks. This is expected behavior, but it relies on external command execution and a referenced script that was not present in the scanned file tree.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct evidence of external command use. Confidence is medium-high because the scanned package only contains Markdown instructions, not executable code to inspect.

medium_findings.title: Gemini API Key Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill requires GEMINI_API_KEY and suggests using an environment variable or .env file. This is legitimate for Gemini access, but secret material can be exposed through local files, subprocess environments, or logs if handled carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires an API key and documents .env usage. No evidence found that the key is exfiltrated, so this is a handling risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Research Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed around Google Gemini Deep Research and links users to Google AI Studio. Research queries may be sent to an external AI service when the referenced workflow is implemented.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network dependency is clear from the source URL and Gemini setup instructions. The exact request behavior cannot be confirmed because no implementation file was available.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography near the description and Exit Codes heading. No cryptographic algorithm use was found at those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive metadata and a section heading, not cryptographic code. This makes the static alerts very likely false positives.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged network reconnaissance at the success exit code line. That line documents exit code 0 and does not contain scanning or probing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line only describes a success exit code. No evidence found for host discovery, port scanning, or network probing.

low_findings.title: Critical Dangerous Combination Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The combined code execution, network, and credential pattern appears in Markdown setup and usage instructions. No executable implementation was available in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous combination exists as documented workflow text, not as confirmed malicious code. Human caution is still warranted because the referenced script was not available for review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides shell commands for dependency installation and research execution. Users should inspect any referenced script before running it with credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in Markdown code blocks and are intended to be run. The risk depends on external files that were not present in this scanned package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Secret Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires a GEMINI_API_KEY and documents .env use. Users should avoid committing the file and should scope the key to the minimum needed access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential requirement is explicit. No evidence found of direct key exposure beyond local configuration instructions.

content.value_statement: Research tasks often require many searches, source reviews, and synthesis passes. This skill guides Gemini-based research workflows that produce structured, cited reports.
actual_capabilities: Documents how to start a Gemini research task from a query.
Shows optional structured report formats for comparative research.
Describes streaming, status, wait, list, and continuation workflows.
Supports markdown reports, structured output, and raw response modes.
Estimates task duration, cost, and token usage for planning.
limitations: The scanned package contains only SKILL.md and no research script implementation.
Requires a Gemini API key and likely sends queries to a third-party service.
Research quality depends on Gemini output and source availability.
Costs and latency can vary by query complexity and provider behavior.

use_cases.title: Market Landscape Brief
use_cases.description: Produce a structured market overview with competitors, trends, risks, and open questions.

use_cases.title: Technical Literature Review
use_cases.description: Summarize research papers, technical articles, and implementation tradeoffs for a focused topic.

use_cases.title: Due Diligence Research
use_cases.description: Gather cited evidence about a company, technology, or vendor before a decision.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Topic Research
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a first-pass cited overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research this topic with the deep-research skill: [topic]. Include key facts, sources, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when comparing products, companies, or frameworks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [option A], [option B], and [option C]. Cover strengths, weaknesses, pricing, adoption, and recommendation criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Market Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the output must follow a business report format.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the [market] market. Format the report with summary, market size, key players, trends, risks, and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Follow-Up Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after an initial research run needs deeper analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue the previous research on [interaction id]. Expand the evidence for [specific point] and identify conflicting sources.

output_examples.output: A cited timeline covering Borg origins, Kubernetes release milestones, CNCF adoption, ecosystem growth, and current governance.

output: Executive summary with recommended choices by project type.
Comparison of Django, Flask, FastAPI, and other frameworks.
Tradeoffs for performance, ecosystem, learning curve, and deployment.

output_examples.input: Analyze the electric vehicle battery market.
output_examples.output: A market brief with demand drivers, major suppliers, chemistry trends, regional risks, and cited investment signals.

best_practices: Review the referenced script before running commands with an API key.
Use narrow research questions and request a specific report structure.
Verify important claims against primary sources before making decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill with confidential data unless provider sharing is approved.
Do not treat generated citations as verified without checking the sources.
Do not store GEMINI_API_KEY in files that can be committed or shared.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides Gemini-based research tasks for market analysis, literature reviews, comparisons, and due diligence.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documentation requires GEMINI_API_KEY for Gemini access.

faq.question: Is the implementation included?
faq.answer: No. The scanned package contains SKILL.md only, so the referenced script was not audited.

faq.question: Can it produce cited reports?
faq.answer: The documentation says it produces detailed, cited reports when the referenced workflow is available.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should inspect the referenced script, dependency file, environment handling, and provider data policies.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with a warning because the risks are documented workflow risks, not confirmed malicious behavior.
